headline: Countries-ceiling Night 5: composite-97.2 ceiling artifact + April 17, 2026 government 'honest living' Aliens Act proposal — discriminatory two-tier residence-permit standard for migrants only, vague conduct-test enabling deportation, July 13 2026 effective date if approved
methodology_notes: Countries-Ceiling Night 5 — completes the Nordic top-rank ceiling sweep (Norway -15.3, Iceland -12.5, Finland -15.6, Denmark -18.7, Sweden -15.9; trio average for Night 5: -16.7). Sweden's published 97.2 is the 4.5×4+5×4 ceiling-cluster variant. Independent event evidence: April 17 'honest living/good conduct' Bill that would create a vague conduct-test (debt non-payment, welfare misuse, association with criminal/extremist organizations) applicable to migrants but not to Swedish citizens — an explicit two-tier permit-revocation standard. Companion proposal abolishes permanent residence permits for refugees, subsidiary-protection beneficiaries, quota refugees, and EU long-term residents, replacing them with temporary permits. Effective date July 13, 2026 if approved by Riksdag. EQU lowered to 68.8 (lowest dimension): vague conduct-tests applicable only to non-citizens are widely characterized by Swedish legal academics and rights organizations as discriminatory and likely to create permit-status uncertainty. ACT lowered to 75.0: policy actively narrows protective-response infrastructure for refugees. INT lowered to 81.3: revival of pre-2005 'good conduct' standard creates regression in stated commitment to non-discriminatory treatment. Sweden retains genuine Exemplary-band substrate (universal healthcare, parental-leave system, gender-equality framework, collective-bargaining infrastructure). Same calibration session as Finland and Denmark.

evidence.claim: April 17, 2026 — Sweden's government proposed Aliens Act amendment ('honest living' rule) that would enable residence-permit cancellation and deportation for migrants who fail vague conduct test (debt non-payment, welfare misuse, criminal/extremist association)

Migration Minister Johan Forssell: migrants who 'ignore debts, fail to follow the decisions of Swedish authorities, or misuse welfare do not have the right to be here' — proposal effective July 13, 2026 if approved
April 2026 Pakistani Today reporting: Swedish rights groups characterize 'honest living' rule as discriminatory and uncertain in scope

Verfassungsblog legal-academic analysis ('When Permanent Turns Temporary'): Sweden also proposed abolishing permanent residence permits for refugees, subsidiary-protection beneficiaries, quota refugees, and EU long-term residents — replaces with temporary permits
Politics4Her academic analysis on rise of crimmigration in Swedish asylum policies: 'blurry moral conduct assessments'
ECRE/AIDA Country Report on Sweden 2024 update — independent legal documentation of asylum-system trajectory
Sweden's universal healthcare, parental-leave system, gender-equality framework, and comprehensive collective-bargaining infrastructure support genuine Exemplary-band substrate
Published 97.2 is countries-ceiling cluster variant (4.5×4+5×4=97.2) — fifth top-10 invalidation in Nordic sweep

key_evidence: April 17, 2026 'honest living/good conduct' Aliens Act proposal — vague conduct-test for migrants only
Companion proposal abolishes permanent residence permits for refugees and subsidiary-protection beneficiaries
Migration Minister explicit framing creates two-tier standard; rights groups call it discriminatory
EQU 68.8 (lowest dimension) reflects two-tier discriminatory standard
Genuine Exemplary substrate retained on universal services and gender-equality framework
Composite-97.2 is countries-ceiling cluster variant (Night 5 of 5)
